interpretation: d alone explains only part of H (mean |residual| = 0.170). The hierarchical topic structure (sigma_eta) and AR(1) component (phi) contribute significantly. Compositionally, d reflects the DEPTH of thematic nesting: texts where themes are embedded within larger themes (e.g., AT: verse → pericope → chapter → book-section) have higher d. The Rig Veda (d ≈ 0) has essentially flat structure (meter-driven, no thematic hierarchy). NT (d = 0.195) has the deepest nesting (epistle structure with digressions, argument → sub-argument → example → conclusion). AT (d = 0.128) and Corán (d = 0.109) are intermediate: strong thematic hierarchy but less nested than epistolary structure.
